items.title: 21yo man stabbed to death in crowd in Tennant Creek street

children.content: Police have arrested a 17-year-old boy after a man was fatally stabbed in a street in Tennant Creek overnight.

image.altText: Tennant creek from the top of a hill.

items.title: NT government axes controversial $1.1 million private midwife contract

children.content: The Northern Territory government has axed a $1.1 million contract awarded to a Darwin midwife, banned from working unsupervised, almost 10 months after doctors began raising concerns about the deal.

image.altText: A woman in a brown dress looks at the camera.

items.title: Alleged drug dealer caught after trading cannabis for cat food

children.content: Police charge a 45-year-old woman with a range of drug, weapons and other offences after a group of teenagers were caught stealing cat food to allegedly swap with her for cannabis.

image.altText: A large collection of bags full of cannabis, laid out on a carpet.

items.title: NT government says Claravale land-clearing dispute is now settled

children.content: For years, the Northern Territory government has been locked in a legal battle with Claravale Station over allegations its owners cleared hundreds of hectares without a permit. But it is not the first time the government has announced the settlement deal.

image.altText: Cleared land with two lone trees in the middle.

items.title: Creditors of NT mine with $360m debt criticise lack of oversight

children.content: In the wake of Nathan River Resources entering into voluntary administration, creditors have criticised the NT government for its lack of oversight of the struggling company. ASIC documents show the mining company owes more than $360 million.

articles.title: Iron ore company 'undermines' promises after failing to pay traditional owners

children.content: Nathan River Resources went into administration and owes about $360 million to creditors including the NT government and traditional owners.

image.altText: A digger is filling up a truck with iron ore in the NT.

gallery.altText: A man standing with his arms folded, in front of several long metal pipes in an outdoor work yard.

thumbnail.altText: A man standing with his arms folded, in front of several long metal pipes in an outdoor work yard.

avatar.alt: Jane Bardon in a blazer looking at the camera.

items.title: Police dog puts alleged fugitive bikie in hospital

children.content: A Top End bikie who allegedly escaped police custody last week has been hospitalised after being bitten by a police dog during his re-capture.

image.altText: A dog stands on the back seat of a ute.

items.title: NT government questioned over youth prison lockdowns, police policy

children.content: As the NT's CLP government remains firm on its tough-on-crime agenda and rebuilding of the economy, the first week of budget estimates saw the government questioned around prison lockdowns, police policy and housing.

image.altText: Two curved tables facing each other, people sitting either side, most in formal office wear.

items.title: NT attorney-general backs community safety changes despite unrest

children.content: The government says bail law changes made a year ago are working despite a string of tragedies and unrest in bush communities.

gallery.altText: Close-up of a white woman, blonde hair, wearing a light blue blazer, white blouse, looking intently at camera, soft smile.

thumbnail.altText: Close-up of a white woman, blonde hair, wearing a light blue blazer, white blouse, looking intently at camera, soft smile.

items.title: Man sentenced to 10 years in jail for stabbing death of niece in Darwin

children.content: Andrew Mungatopi has been sentenced to 10 years and two months in prison for the fatal stabbing of his niece in a Darwin backyard in 2024.

image.altText: A First Nations man wearing mirrored sunglasses, pointing at himself.

items.title: Three hospitalised as Central Australian family dispute turns violent

children.content: Three people have been hospitalised after a family dispute at a Central Australian outstation turned violent over the weekend, sparking fears of further unrest.

image.altText: Two women, looking serious, sitting on chairs inside a studio.

items.title: Two killed after Taliban opens fire on protest against women violating dress code

children.content: People protested after morality police arrested women for not wearing the body-cloaking chador or burqa. Then the Taliban cracked down.

gallery.altText: A woman, wearing a burqa, peeks out of a curtain.

thumbnail.altText: A woman, wearing a burqa, peeks out of a curtain.

items.title: Billionaire says $150m court loss no reason to change approach to native title

children.content: Mining magnate Andrew "Twiggy" Forrest has defended his company's approach to Indigenous agreements after a court ordered the miner to pay $150 million in compensation to the Yindjibarndi people.

image.altText: Andrew Forrest stands at the front of an auditorium holding a microphone and wearing a beige suit jacket.

items.title: NDIS labour hire company enters administration, 480 jobs in limbo

children.content: NDIS labour hire provider United Foundation has entered voluntary administration blaming regulatory changes, delayed payments and rising costs.

image.altText: A man in a wheelchair holding a cane next to someone walking and wearing jeans.

items.title: World Cup quick hits: Opening ceremony delights, drinks break drama and Haiti's jersey ban

children.content: The 2026 FIFA World Cup is up and running, launched by a colourful opening ceremony and comprehensive win for Mexico. Here are the quick hits from the opening day of the tournament.

articles.title: South Korea completes Czechia comeback, relief for Socceroos star

children.content: The 2026 FIFA World Cup gets underway as South Korea comes from a goal down to beat Czechia 2-1 and Mexico defeats South Africa 2-0 in an opening game which features three red cards.

image.altText: South Korea's Hwang In-beom is hugged by Seol Young-woo in celebration of a goal

articles.title: Wild scenes in Mexico City as fans fill the streets after World Cup opener

children.content: Thousands of Mexican fans spill onto the streets of Mexico City after El Tri's opening World Cup triumph against South Africa.

image.altText: A young man has an open mouth as he prepares to shout and lift a trophy

articles.title: Socceroos coach Tony Popovic signs extension through to Asian Cup

children.content: Socceroos coach Tony Popovic gets his contract extended just days before Australia's World Cup kicks off to take the national team through to next year's Asian Cup.

image.altText: Socceroos coach Tony Popovic gestures to his team, holding his arm out in a halting motion during a game.

gallery.altText: A shot showing fireworks, dancers and a giant replica World Cup trophy during the Opening Ceremony

thumbnail.altText: A shot showing fireworks, dancers and a giant replica World Cup trophy during the Opening Ceremony

items.title: Cats continue alarming Suns slide as Hardwick lashes 'ridiculous' rule

children.content: Geelong records a 45-point win over Gold Coast as Suns coach Damien Hardwick tees off at the officiating.

image.altText: An AFL player in white celebrates a goal by jumping up and down

items.title: Federal government has contracts worth more than $650m with embattled KPMG

children.content: Parliamentary library data reveals the federal government has almost 300 active contracts with accounting firm KPMG, which is being investigated over claims it misused confidential client information.

image.altText: A large, grey building with a blue and white sign at the top reading KPMG.

items.title: Gus Lamont's grandparent fined over gun offence, unrelated to 4yo's disappearance

children.content: The grandparent of missing four-year-old Gus Lamont has been fined $10,500 for possession of a gun silencer at the family's Oak Park Station, in South Australia's far north. Police have previously said the offence is not related to his disappearance.

image.altText: Josie Murray bumping into reporters outside Adelaide Magistrates Court.

gallery.altText: A person wearing with shoulder-length blonde hair wearing an Akubra hat and maroon jacket, holding a black handbag

thumbnail.altText: A person wearing with shoulder-length blonde hair wearing an Akubra hat and maroon jacket, holding a black handbag

avatar.alt: A woman with blonde hair wearing a black blazer and white top

items.title: Viral lookout reopens after tourists used yards as 'toilets'

children.content: Lincolns Rock shut after crowds flocked to the site to recreate a photo posted on Instagram of a K-pop star dangling her legs off the sandstone cliff.

image.altText: Tourists sit and stand on rock face overlooking the Blue Mountains Valley.

items.title: Why this fashion designer sells her clothes on social media not shops

children.content: Katharina Mildren's clothes have attracted legions of fans online and she ships items around the world, but there is one place you will not find her garments: in a shop.

image.altText: Young woman with dark hair pulled back away from her face. She is wearing a blue checked dress with puffy sleeves.

items.title: What are the rules around using fire pits at home?

children.content: There are rules when it comes to using fire pits and things to be cautious about, including open-air fires being a nuisance to your neighbours.

avatar.alt: Kellie Scott black and white headshot

items.title: Roxane Gay and Channing Tatum join forces thanks to this Australian

children.content: It's possible the ball may not have got rolling on Roxane Gay and Channing Tatum's upcoming novel if it wasn't for an Australian connection. Gay will appear at Vivid Sydney in a wide-ranging discussion.

image.altText: Roxane Gay with hair in black and grey braids tied in a bun looking out a window.

items.title: NDIS spending cuts would see 240,000 people exit the scheme, inquiry told

children.content: More than 240,000 people would exit the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS) over four years according to modelling as part of a plan to cut spending on the multi-billion-dollar program.

image.altText: Jenny mcalliser appearing at Estimates Committee

items.title: Independence leader vanishes after changing sides to support Indonesia's government

children.content: A key Indigenous leader in West Papua appeared in a documentary criticising an Indonesian government program, but then changed sides and disappeared. Her family suspects foul play.

gallery.altText: Film poster depicting a woman in traditional Papuan clothing with a red cross in the background looking at the camera

thumbnail.altText: Film poster depicting a woman in traditional Papuan clothing with a red cross in the background looking at the camera

items.title: Ex pro cyclist Rohan Dennis reported for driving while disqualified, ABC understands

children.content: Rohan Dennis was handed a suspended sentence last year for the events leading up to the death of his Olympian wife Melissa Hoskins in December 2023.

image.altText: A white sedan on the tray of a tow truck parked in front of a police car

items.title: Government agencies fail first hurdle under AI self-reporting policy

children.content: The ABC can reveal dozens of federal agencies missed mandatory deadlines to disclose how they are currently using AI.

image.altText: A woman's hands type on a laptop keyboard.

items.title: How you and your superannuation came to be aboard SpaceX

children.content: If your superannuation is managed by any of Australia's big superannuation funds, chances are you're about to become a highly valued financial backer to Elon Musk.

gallery.altText: smoke and flames emerge from a spacex launch in texas

thumbnail.altText: smoke and flames emerge from a spacex launch in texas

items.title: Greens say premier's answers about legal fees 'paint a far worse picture'

children.content: Tasmanian Premier Jeremy Rockliff has provided answers to a series of questions about former minister Madeleine Ogilvie's legal fees matter, but the Greens say they "paint a far worse picture of this shameful saga and his role in it".

image.altText: Jeremy Rockliff walks into a room at Government House, surrounded by three other men wearing suits.

items.title: No sympathy for ‘juvenile grubs’ in detention centres, Qld deputy premier says

children.content: Jarrod Bleijie says children inside youth detention centres are largely to blame for lockdowns, and it's the staff that need protection.

image.altText: a man talking into a bank of microphones

items.title: Murderer's mum uses ChatGPT to tell court of son's 'caring nature'

children.content: The mother of a man found guilty of murdering underworld figure Gavin Preston has told the Supreme Court her son has always been polite and considerate.

image.altText: A close-up selfie of a man in a car.

items.title: Air India crash plunged victims' families 'into darkness'

children.content: One year on from the fatal Air India crash that claimed 260 lives, families of victims are still waiting for a report into the investigation.

image.altText: A schreenshot of a couple from a video call.

items.title: Unsecured creditors of iconic music festival to get 11c in the dollar

children.content: The iconic Byron Bay music festival may have been trading while insolvent for months before it collapsed, according to a new liquidator's report.

gallery.altText: Front on image of an audience at Bluesfest. A big crowd of smiling people with their hands in the air in a blue tent.

thumbnail.altText: Front on image of an audience at Bluesfest. A big crowd of smiling people with their hands in the air in a blue tent.

avatar.alt: Photo of ABC Newcastle investigative reporter Alex Turner-Cohen, smiling and wearing a white shirt, arms folded

items.title: Court hears of 'tragic mistake' that caused fatal Canberra crash

children.content: The daughters of a motorcyclist killed in Canberra's north tried to reach him for their nightly phone call as he lay dying on the road, an ACT court has heard.

image.altText: A man with a beard smiles, wearing a hat that says Harley Davidson, sitting inside an eatery.

items.title: Parton says Canberrans are 'more vulnerable' after budget's release

children.content: The Canberra Liberals and the ACT Greens issue their responses to the ACT budget, criticising the government's fiscal plan, principally around cost of living, transport and overall management of the territory's finances.

image.altText: Mark Parton and Leanne Castley

items.title: Thai princess dies aged 47 after spending years in a coma

children.content: Princess Bajrakitiyabha Narendira Debyavati, who had spent the past three and a half years in a coma due to a heart condition, is being remembered for her efforts to improve the livelihoods of female prisoners and her diplomatic career.

gallery.altText: A formally dressed middle-aged Thai woman smiles as she leans down to greet members of a crowd.

thumbnail.altText: A formally dressed middle-aged Thai woman smiles as she leans down to greet members of a crowd.

items.title: Three decades later, 18 soldiers lost in a training crash are not forgotten

children.content: During counterterrorism training in 1996, two Black Hawk helicopters collided. Eighteen men were killed, making it Australia's worst peacetime military aviation disaster.

image.altText: Graphic showing a helicopter, crash remnants, and tributes
